scope_note_md: Scope: API inbound contract only — request header, request fields, response fields, error response fields. DB and outbound sections are covered in sibling reports.

scenarios.description: missing token → 401, no DB write, no outbound API call, no data leak
malformed token (e.g. "Bearer notarealjwt") → 401, no DB write, no data leak
expired token → 401, no DB write, no data leak
token for deleted/disabled user → 401, no DB write, no data leak

scenarios.partial_note: status only; missing: no DB write / no outbound / no data leak assertions
status only; missing: no DB write / no outbound / no data leak assertions

scenarios.description: zero "0" → 422, no DB write, no data leak
exceeds max (1_000_000 + 1 sat) → 422, no DB write, no data leak
amount > wallet.balance → 422, no DB write, no balance leak
presence + human-readable message + no stack trace leak
ChargeError path → 'Payment processing failed' exact message

contract_map.scenarios_needed: nil, empty string, zero, negative, non-numeric, boundary (min/max), precision, amount==balance, amount>balance, concurrent TOCTOU
presence on 422, human-readable, no stack trace leak, no balance leak, IDOR non-disclosure, ChargeError exact message

gaps.description: Error response `details` array currently includes raw balance in the InsufficientBalanceError path. No test asserts that sensitive financial data is NOT leaked in error responses. This is a FINTECH security gap — error-path contract must forbid balance disclosure.
gaps.stub:
context 'when amount exceeds balance' do
  let(:amount) { '10000' }
  it 'returns 422 and does not leak balance' do
    expect { run_test }.not_to change(Transaction, :count)
    body = JSON.parse(response.body)
    expect(body['details'].join(' ')).not_to include(WALLET_BALANCE)
  end
end

gaps.description: No test exercises the IDOR scenario — a valid wallet_id belonging to a different user. Controller uses `current_user.wallets.find_by(...)` but without a test any future refactor would silently permit cross-tenant writes.
gaps.stub:
context 'when wallet_id belongs to another user' do
  let(:other_user) { create(:user) }
  let(:wallet_id)  { create(:wallet, user: other_user).id }
  it 'returns 422 and does not disclose other user wallet' do
    expect { run_test }.not_to change(Transaction, :count)
    expect(response).to have_http_status(:unprocessable_entity)
  end
end

gaps.description: No 401 test at all. Every request spec assumes a valid auth header. Missing/malformed/expired tokens must all return 401 with no DB write.
gaps.stub:
context 'without Authorization header' do
  let(:headers) { {} }
  it 'returns 401 and writes nothing' do
    expect { run_test }.not_to change(Transaction, :count)
    expect(response).to have_http_status(:unauthorized)
  end
end

gaps.description: No test for amount=0. `numericality greater_than: 0` means zero must be rejected with 422.
gaps.stub:
context 'when amount is zero' do
  let(:amount) { '0' }
  it 'rejects with 422' do
    expect(response).to have_http_status(:unprocessable_entity)
  end
end

gaps.description: description is a free-text string persisted to DB. SQL/XSS/command/NULL-byte payloads should all be stored verbatim (not executed).

test_stubs_md:
RSpec pseudocode for HIGH/CRITICAL gaps. Uses the `run_test`/`defaults` foundation the audit recommended.

```ruby
RSpec.describe 'POST /api/v1/transactions', type: :request do
  subject(:run_test) do
    post '/api/v1/transactions', params: { transaction: params }, headers: headers, as: :json
  end
  # see per-gap stubs above
end
```
